name: Goldman Sachs Bank Usa
description_short: Goldman Sachs Bank USA operates Marcus, an online-only savings platform offering high-yield savings accounts, CDs, and no-penalty CDs with FDIC insurance and competitive APY rates.
description_long: Goldman Sachs Bank USA is a legitimate, FDIC-insured bank headquartered at the Salt Lake City Branch, operating under the Marcus by Goldman Sachs brand. The company specializes exclusively in retail deposit products delivered through a digital-first platform, with no physical branches. Marcus launched as Goldman Sachs' consumer banking division to serve retail customers seeking savings solutions rather than investment products. The company offers three primary savings vehicles: Online Savings Accounts with no minimum balance requirement, High-Yield CDs with 9-month terms, and No-Penalty CDs with 11-month terms. All products carry FDIC insurance protection up to applicable limits. Current rates as of April 2026 include 3.65% APY on savings accounts, 4.05% on high-yield CDs, and 3.95% on no-penalty CDs, with maximum balance limits that apply. The mobile app provides account management and balance tracking. Marcus differentiates itself through Goldman Sachs' institutional backing, straightforward product offering without investment complexity, and competitive yield rates relative to traditional banks. The no-penalty CD specifically addresses the early-withdrawal constraint typical of fixed-term products. However, the platform lacks checking accounts, debit cards, and lending products, limiting it to savers rather than customers seeking comprehensive banking services. Maximum balance restrictions, while unstated in specifics, represent an operational constraint not typical of traditional banks. The company's educational resources address savings strategy and financial awareness. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online Savings Account with 3.65% APY and no minimum balance
High-Yield CD with 9-month terms at 4.05% APY
No-Penalty CD with 11-month terms at 3.95% APY and 7-day withdrawal access
FDIC deposit insurance protection on all accounts
Mobile app for account access and balance tracking
Online account opening and account management
Educational resources on savings accounts, CDs, and financial planning
Multiple CD term options beyond the three featured products
Automatic interest crediting based on stated APY
Digital-only banking with no physical branch locations
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by Goldman Sachs' institutional strength and U.S. government protection
Competitive APY rates: 3.65% on savings, 4.05% on 9-month CDs, 3.95% on no-penalty CDs as of April 2026
No minimum balance requirement for Online Savings Account, lowering barrier to entry
No-Penalty CD allows full withdrawal after 7 days, unusual flexibility for fixed-term products
Low $500 minimum to open and earn stated APY on CD products
Mobile app provides convenient account access and balance management
APY rates transparent and clearly disclosed with effective dates
cons: Maximum balance limits apply across all products, limiting usefulness for high-net-worth savers
No checking accounts, debit cards, or lending services—savings-only platform with limited functionality
APY rates subject to change at any time before and after account opening, offering no rate guarantees
No-Penalty CD requires full balance withdrawal; partial withdrawals are not permitted
Limited product breadth compared to traditional banks; no investment, mortgage, or credit services
best_for: Conservative savers seeking FDIC-insured high-yield savings with no account minimums
Customers wanting fixed-term CD products with institutional backing and competitive rates
Individuals prioritizing digital-only banking and mobile account management over branch services
Savers below the maximum balance threshold seeking penalty-free early withdrawal options
diagnosis: Marcus by Goldman Sachs is best for conservative savers and CD investors who want institutional credibility, competitive rates, and FDIC insurance without needing checking accounts, lending, or investment products. The primary caveat is that maximum balance limits restrict utility for larger savers, and APY rates fluctuate, so yield advantages are not permanent.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Goldman Sachs operates Marcus, offering high-yield savings accounts, CDs, and no-penalty CDs with FDIC insurance.
